name: Prose: "Seventeen Oranges" — Bill Naughton

sourceRefs.label: 2025_q1 page 6 Q8 (fill-in-the-blanks: forced to swallow chunks/pips, with answer key)
civilserviceaspirants.in - dedicated MCQ set (6 questions); brainkart.com - full plot summary

researchNotes: 1 real past-paper hit (a fill-in-the-blanks word-order question) plus a clean 6-question web-research MCQ set — together they cover the story well. A comic tale of a narrator who, caught red-handed with stolen oranges, destroys the evidence by eating all 17 of them whole (peels, pips, chunks) on the spot.

reading.intro: A humorous first-person story about a cart-driver for the Swift Delivery Company who is extremely fond of oranges, gets caught by a dock security officer ('Pongo') with 17 stolen oranges hidden in his apron — and, to avoid being caught with the evidence, eats all of them whole on the spot, peels/pips/chunks included, and pays the price with a week of stomach trouble.

qa.question: Who wrote "Seventeen Oranges", and what was the narrator's job?
qa.answer: Bill Naughton. The narrator was a cart driver for the Swift Delivery Company, driving a pony-and-cart in and out of the docks to deliver goods.

qa.question: What was the narrator especially fond of, and how did he usually hide them?
qa.answer: Oranges — he was very fond of eating them. He usually hid stolen oranges in his APRON.

qa.question: Who caught the narrator, how many oranges was he caught with, and why was the officer suspicious?
qa.answer: A dock security officer named Pongo caught him, with SEVENTEEN oranges found in his pockets/apron. Pongo was suspicious because too many people were stealing from the docks at the time.

qa.question: Real exam: Complete the sequence — "Children like to ___ crispy snacks. But the author... was very fond of ___ oranges. Eventually, he was forced to ___ all the ___ including ___."
qa.answer: "Children like to MUNCH crispy snacks. But the author... was very fond of EATING oranges. Eventually, he was forced to SWALLOW all the CHUNKS including PIPS" — confirmed by a real exam's fill-in-the-blanks question.

qa.question: Why was the narrator "forced" to swallow all the orange chunks and pips whole, and what happened to him afterward?
qa.answer: To destroy the evidence and avoid being caught red-handed with the stolen oranges by Pongo, he hurriedly ate/swallowed all 17 oranges whole — peels, pips, and all. As a result, he felt very sick for a week afterward, as the oranges 'worked away' in his stomach.

options: Cart driver for the Swift Delivery Company
questions.explanation: Cart driver for the Swift Delivery Company.

options: To destroy the evidence before being searched

questions.prompt: What happened to the narrator after swallowing all the oranges whole?
options: He felt very sick for a week
